mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically elevating equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, directly reducing corporate profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances despite the high unemployment signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand expansion, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive quarterly forward returns for the S&P 500.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in retail and housing fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.96 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening factory output and supply chain utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of observations during expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 232.0, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL crosses the 232.0 threshold, with no statistically significant drawdown observed.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, which supports consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to yield positive 20-day returns in >65% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that directly boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production falls below 95.0, it signals severe manufacturing contraction and supply-side bottlenecks that weigh on corporate output, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where industrial production drops below the 95.0 threshold.
